name: Ph.D. in Data Science

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: For a doctoral program the sampled courses form a reasonable research-preparation set spanning advanced ML (DS 675, CS 732 Advanced Machine Learning), high-performance/parallel computing (CS 668 Parallel Algorithms, DS 642 Applications of Parallel Computing), advanced statistics (MATH 644 Regression, MATH 786 Large Sample Theory at depth 4, MATH 787 Non-Parametric Statistics), plus research scaffolding (DS 791 Graduate Seminar, DS 792B Pre-Doctoral Research, DS 725/726 Independent Study). However requirementGroups=0, so the page shows a course pool rather than an organized structure of breadth requirements, qualifying-exam coursework, and dissertation milestones.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish a structured doctoral roadmap (required breadth coursework, qualifying exam, candidacy, dissertation research) instead of an undifferentiated course pool.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The graph has strong depth (deepestPrereqChain=4, e.g. reaching MATH 786 Large Sample Theory), which is appropriate for a Ph.D., but missingCourseCount=7 with dangling references including DS 700, DS 701, DS 790A, MATH 700, MATH 701 (dissertation/research courses) and CS 280, CS 435 (lower-level references). Some danglings are explainable research-course codes, yet as published they are unresolved.
dimensions.suggestion: Resolve or annotate the seven dangling codes (DS 700/701/790A, MATH 700/701, CS 280/435) so every referenced course either resolves or is clearly marked as a research/registration placeholder.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No doctoral program outcomes or objectives (e.g., ability to conduct original research, disseminate results, pass qualifying milestones) are present in the data; only a course list is exposed.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish Ph.D.-level outcomes tied to the seminar (DS 791) and pre-doctoral research (DS 792B), covering original research, peer-reviewed dissemination, and independent scholarship.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits, summedTermCredits, and creditReconcileDelta are all null, which is expected for a Ph.D. and not a hard defect, but the page still gives no view of the coursework-credit expectation before candidacy or how dissertation credits are structured, so distribution is opaque.
dimensions.suggestion: State the coursework credit requirement, seminar expectation (DS 791), and dissertation-credit milestones so doctoral students can plan even though a single fixed total does not apply.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: Course descriptions are substantive and the research/independent-study courses are clearly labeled, but with requirementGroups=0 there is no program overview or milestone structure, seven references dangle, and DS 791 appears at 0 credits without context, so the page reads as a list rather than a doctoral program map.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a program-structure section (milestones, timeline, advising) around the course pool and footnote the 0-credit seminar and research placeholders.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The research-relevant content is current: high-performance and parallel computing (CS 668, DS 642), advanced and trustworthy ML (CS 732, DS 789 Trustworthy Artificial Intelligence), big data (DS 644), data visualization (DS 650), and a rigorous statistics spine (MATH 644/786/787) collectively track the modern data-science research frontier.
dimensions.suggestion: Add or flag an elective on large-scale/foundation-model methods so doctoral breadth reflects current ML research as well as classical statistics and HPC.

descriptionQuality.justification: Descriptions are informative and specific for the substantive courses (e.g., CS 668 details shared-memory and special-architecture parallel algorithms; MATH 786 lists limit theorems, asymptotic expansions, and martingale results), though several entries are research/registration courses whose text is largely administrative boilerplate (DS 725/726, DS 792B, MATH 725/726).

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites are explicit and sensible where present (CS 668 'CS 610 and CS 650'; MATH 786 'MATH 665 and MATH 768'; MATH 644 'MATH 661'), but missingCourseCount=7 means several referenced prerequisites and research courses are unresolved, and many research/independent-study courses list no prerequisite at all.

missingTopics.rationale: Beyond DS 791 Graduate Seminar, no explicit course on research design, reproducibility, or ethics is shown, which doctoral programs are expected to require.
CS 732 Advanced Machine Learning is present but no dedicated deep-learning or representation-learning course appears in the doctoral sample, a gap for a modern data-science Ph.D.
The statistics/HPC spine (MATH 786, CS 668) presumes advanced optimization, yet no dedicated optimization course is listed in the pool.
With a strong classical-statistics core (MATH 644/786/787), the absence of causal-inference coursework is a notable omission for contemporary data-science research.

emergingTrends.rationale: Pairing CS 732 and DS 642 with a foundation-model methods course would align doctoral breadth with the dominant direction of ML research.
DS 789 Trustworthy Artificial Intelligence is a strong seed that can anchor a doctoral concentration on fairness, robustness, and interpretability research.
The HPC courses (CS 668, DS 642) position the program to add scientific-ML research bridging simulation and learning.

emergingTrends.trend: Reproducible, open, and large-scale data science
emergingTrends.rationale: Doctoral training increasingly emphasizes reproducibility and open science; a methods course would modernize the DS 791 seminar backbone.

topSuggestions: Publish a structured doctoral roadmap: breadth coursework, qualifying exam, candidacy, and dissertation milestones.
Resolve or annotate the seven dangling course references (DS 700/701/790A, MATH 700/701, CS 280/435).
Add explicit research-methods and responsible-conduct-of-research requirements beyond the seminar.
Publish Ph.D.-level learning outcomes and dissertation-credit expectations.
Broaden ML breadth with a foundation-model or deep-learning-theory elective alongside the classical statistics spine.
